{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56456","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56456/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56456/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56456/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56456","id":2634992635,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6dDsv7","number":56456,"title":"`mean(skipmissing(x))` is imprecise","user":{"login":"tiemvanderdeure","id":124562211,"node_id":"U_kgDOB2yrIw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/124562211?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/tiemvanderdeure","html_url":"https://github.com/tiemvanderdeure","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tiemvanderdeure/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tiemvanderdeure/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tiemvanderdeure/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tiemvanderdeure/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tiemvanderdeure/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tiemvanderdeure/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tiemvanderdeure/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tiemvanderdeure/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tiemvanderdeure/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-11-05T10:03:09Z","updated_at":"2024-11-05T10:03:09Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"The generic fallback for `mean` can give wrong results in some cases. Maybe it would be a good idea to add a specialized implementation when it is called on a `SkipMissing`, as there is for `sum`. The easiest way to do this would be to pass a function that computes both `sum` and `count` to `mapreduce`.

```julia
A = rand(Float32, 1_000_000) .+ 1f6
mean(skipmissing(A)) ≈ mean(A) # false
mean(A) ≈ (sum(skipmissing(A)) / length(A)) # true
```

Related: https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/30421","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56456/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56456/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

isn't working"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-10-17T13:07:28Z","updated_at":"2024-10-17T13:07:28Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug 🐞**
Iterating over a `DataLoader` is still broken on 4.0.3. If `epochs` is less than the number of elements in `DataLoader`, only the first `epochs` elements get evaluated.

**Expected behavior**
Surely this should iterate over the entire dataset `epochs` times

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**
```julia
function lossf(θ, data)
    @show data
    return sum(θ.^2)
end
dataloader = DataLoader(collect(1:10), batchsize = 1)
opt_func = OptimizationFunction(
    lossf,
    Optimization.AutoZygote())
opt_prob = OptimizationProblem(opt_func, [2.0], dataloader)
res_adam = solve(
    opt_prob, Optimisers.Adam(0.001); epochs = 3)

```

**Error & Stacktrace ⚠️**
No stacktrace, but julia outputs this:
```julia
data = [1]
data = [2]
data = [3]
data = [3]
data = [1]
data = [2]
data = [3]
data = [3]
data = [1]
data = [2]
data = [3]
data = [3]
retcode: Default
u: 1-element Vector{Float64}:
 1.9940003690339771
```

**Environment (please complete the following information):**
Latest versions of Optimization and OptimizationOptimisers

**Additional context**
The problem is in this line:
https://github.com/SciML/Optimization.jl/blob/f6dd24d493def6aadd0c09aa249f09f14086c974/lib/OptimizationOptimisers/src/OptimizationOptimisers.jl#L115
Probably the if condition should be something like `epoch == maxiters && i == length(data)`. It would also be good to add a test.

It only breaks the inner loop, meaning iteration through `dataloader` is reset, but the solver continues on the next epoch.

To the example above I add the following callback:
```julia
function callback(state, l)
    if state.iter % 10 == 2
        @show state.iter
        println("stopping training!")
        return true
    else
        return false
    end
end
```

Now the output of `solve(opt_prob, Optimisers.Adam(0.001); callback, epochs = 5)` is

```
data = [1]
data = [2]
state.iter = 2
stopping training!
data = [1]
data = [2]
state.iter = 12
stopping training!
data = [1]
data = [2]
state.iter = 22
stopping training!
data = [1]
data = [2]
state.iter = 32
stopping training!
data = [1]
data = [2]
state.iter = 42
stopping training!
retcode: Default
u: 1-element Vector{Float64}:
 1.9950002173334274
```

I would expect this to stop the solver after just seeing the first 2 batches. **Additional context**
The problem is in this line:
https://github.com/SciML/Optimization.jl/blob/f6dd24d493def6aadd0c09aa249f09f14086c974/lib/OptimizationOptimisers/src/OptimizationOptimisers.jl#L115
Probably the if condition should be something like `epoch == maxiters && i == length(data)`. It would also be good to add a test.
